mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding corporate output that translates into higher near-term earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show non-positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that widens equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances during any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based demand contraction that amplifies negative earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days beyond the effect of either variable alone.
failure_condition: Joint decline episodes fail to produce significantly lower 60-day returns than episodes with only one declining series (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) contractions predict negative S&P 500 returns exclusively during late-cycle expansions when the labor market operates near full employment, as job losses then signal imminent recession rather than temporary adjustment.
failure_condition: PAYEMS contractions coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of late-cycle observations over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects accelerating consumer spending that boosts revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.0% acts as a restrictive threshold where further monetary tightening sharply elevates discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances with FEDFUNDS above 2.0% do not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) alongside increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) deliver a dual shock to corporate profitability expectations, generating more negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days than either variable predicts independently.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and UNRATE fails to add explanatory power in a regression of 60-day returns (F-test p-value > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 900 thousand units signals acute housing sector stress that spills over into construction and mortgage credit, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 900k coincide with negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.
